findings.evidence: `Comap.lean` lines 111-116 provide only `comap_bot`; `HopfIdeal.lean` lines 394-446 define and expose binary joins via `sup_toIdeal`, `mem_sup`, and `SemilatticeSup`, with arbitrary suprema at lines 459-530.
findings.fix: Add a public lemma such as `@[simp] theorem comap_sup (I J : HopfIdeal R K) : (I ⊔ J).comap f hf = I.comap f hf ⊔ J.comap f hf` (and the analogous `iSup`/`sSup` form if intended for the complete-sup API).
findings.issue: `comap` only records the bottom case; the characteristic API does not state how surjective inverse image interacts with Hopf-ideal sums, even though `HopfIdeal` already exposes `⊔`/suprema as core operations.

summary: The exposed `comap` surface has the basic membership/order API and no longer exposes the implementation, but it omits the interaction with the existing HopfIdeal join operation.
